question: Which is the most common site for congenital cholesteatoma

options.A: Anterior superior quadrant of tympanic membrane
options.B: Posterior superior quadrant of tympanic membrane
options.C: Anterior inferior quadrant of tympanic membrane.
options.D: posterior inferior quadrant of tympanic membrane

history.message:
Agent1 Round 1: Congenital cholesteatomas, unlike acquired ones which typically occur in the pars flaccida region, arise from pre-existing skin invaginations. The anterior superior quadrant near the malleus head is more commonly affected due to its location proximal to these natural invaginations.
Answer: A
